steps.title: Identify the Excess Protection
steps.content: Lady Howard tells Villars “it is time that [Evelina] should see something of the world” and warns that young people “too rigidly sequestered” develop false expectations—her critique centers on excessive sheltering.

steps.title: Apply the Logic to the Choices
steps.content:
Choice D says he is “overly protective” and that Evelina needs “a greater variety of experiences,” directly mirroring her position.

The others either limit the concern narrowly (A), misread the cause (B), or introduce an unsupported claim about the Mirvans (C).

That matches choice D—D is correct.
